pros: Benefits package is comprehensive: Health benefits in particular offer better than average coverage. Excellent PTO allowance and rollover. More company holidays than most employers.
cons: The management team specific to my area were a tight-knit group who created the team I was on several years prior. Somewhere along the way (years before my arrival), processes by the dozens were breaking and not being mitigated properly. Incoming "individual contributors" (IC's) were getting blamed for issues that started years prior and management continued the cycle of promoting ineptitude within the lower ranks. IC like myself and others on my team would then receive negative performance reviews, which prevented us from transferring to other areas within Deloitte if desired. When I started, my grossly understaffed team had 4 members. 13 months later, I was the only one left. The team was back up to 6 three months later. Fast forward another 6 months, and the team was back down to 2. All but one of the seven who left the team also left Deloitte.

pros: Working at Deloitte offers significant professional advantages. The firm’s global reputation provides strong brand value and credibility that carries weight across industries. Employees gain exposure to large-scale, complex projects that develop both technical and business expertise. Deloitte invests heavily in training, certifications, and structured career development, which supports continuous learning. The collaborative environment allows professionals to work alongside highly skilled colleagues and industry leaders, fostering strong networking opportunities. In addition, client-facing projects provide valuable experience in communication, problem-solving, and stakeholder management.
cons: On the other hand, there are certain challenges to consider when working at Deloitte. The demanding nature of client projects often leads to long working hours and tight deadlines, which can create stress and impact work-life balance. The highly competitive culture can sometimes feel overwhelming, and the firm’s large size introduces layers of bureaucracy that may slow decision-making. In some roles, frequent travel is required, which can be tiring over time. While these aspects can be difficult, they also encourage adaptability, time management, and resilience.

title: Unfortunately, there are no notable positive aspects for Intern Analysts at the moment.
pros: Unfortunately, there are no notable positive aspects for Intern Analysts at the moment.
cons:
Intern Analysts at Deloitte are experiencing unfair treatment due to the existence of separate policies that apply only to them. Unlike their peers who joined as Analysts through campus placements, Intern Analysts—most of whom were hired off-campus—are excluded from many of the benefits provided to full-time employees.

Additionally, despite their intern status, many Intern Analysts are reportedly working longer hours and handling responsibilities comparable to or even exceeding those of full-time Analysts. This situation has created a sense of inequality and partiality within the workplace, which is affecting morale and motivation.

It is important that Deloitte re-evaluates these policies to ensure fair treatment and equal opportunities for all employees, regardless of how they were hired.

pros: Plenty of interesting projects to work on, a lot with decent hours. Pay is fine considering expectations and industry (Government)
cons:
My experience may be anomalous, but it was clear from early on that Deloitte did not know how to manage software projects or products.

When I first joined, I worked on a project where 3 of the teams ended up doing the exact same work. The director overseeing the project seemed unaware of this until teams were demoing their progress.

I've seen people who don't seem to have ever worked on a software oriented project leading operations and development. Staff on projects often don't seem concerned with the impact of their work, they take directions and implement them, even if the results don't add up to anything usable. They don't question instructions, they don't think critically about what is actually needed to produce a usable end-product that users will benefit from using. In short, too many people at Deloitte don't have any common sense - and they hire subcontractors who also don't have any common sense.

There are a handful of gems at Deloitte, but Deloitte has no idea how to value them.

pros: Brand name of the company and brand name of the strategy practice is ok (not great). Project quality is great - on par with MBB/other Tier-2 Strat houses.
cons:
Monitor overall is a small team within Deloitte India. Gurgaon strategy team is one of the most toxic places to work. All because this one partner confuses everyone and in the end the project delivery gets ruined, which again makes it tough for everybody - people have to pay the price.
Strategy consulting is known to be tough anyways - have worked in a few other such orgs myself before/after this (think MBB/Strategy&/Alvarez/Parthenon/LEK/Bain)- but even by that standards this practice is terrible - not because of the work obviously, but because of the toxicity of this one person. Saw folks breaking down in meeting rooms alone, getting frustrated due to confusion and even taking breaks due to extreme mental stress - all of them are folks from top bschools to be clear. People within Deloitte from other teams (non Monitor) often do not know the actual status and might think this could be a great entry into strategy work - one such person tried this - did some short term work with us and realized the horror they will get into if they join here with this Partner and decided (and rightly so) to let it go and escape.

Better to join elsewhere, even if it means lower pay (lower pay is highly unlikely though).

Currently, there is no rational reason to work in Deloitte. The pay has always been lower than competitors, but this was made up for by the slightly better flexible benefits.
